mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent within a single month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE crosses the 5.5 percent threshold, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate broad-based economic acceleration, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in HOUST and INDPRO fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with elevated policy rates force market expectations of a Federal Reserve pivot, which compresses real yields and triggers a positive equity repricing over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not turn positive when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, or the joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects resilient consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are flat or negative when RSAFS shows consistent month-over-month growth, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, it signals manufacturing contraction and weakening aggregate demand, prompting downward revisions to earnings guidance and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 105.0, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms healthy labor demand without wage-push inflation, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint improvement in PAYEMS and stability in UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid real GDP growth paired with accelerating consumer prices triggers stagflation fears, which compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and earnings uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Stagflation fears do not materialize into negative SPX returns when GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.
